head:
Likert scale (1= strongly disagree; 2= somewhat disagree; 3= neither agree nor disagree; 4= somewhat agree; 5= strongly agree).

1. My face recognition ability is worse than most people (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
2. I have always had a bad memory for faces (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
3. I find it noticeably easier to recognise people who have distinctive facial features (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
4. I often mistake people I have met before for strangers (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
5. When I was at school I struggled to recognise my classmates (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
6. When people change their hairstyle; or wear hats; I have problems recognising them (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
7. I sometimes have to warn new people I meet that I am 'bad with faces' (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
8. I find it easy to picture individual faces in my mind (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
9. I am better than most people at putting a 'name to a face' (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
10. Without hearing people's voices I struggle to recognise them (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
11. Anxiety about face recognition has led me to avoid certain social or professional situations (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
12. I have to try harder than other people to memorise faces (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
13. I am very confident in my ability to recognise myself in photographs (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
14. I sometimes find movies hard to follow because of difficulties recognising characters (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
15. My friends and family think I have bad face recognition or bad face memory (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
16. I feel like I frequently offend people by not recognising who they are (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
17. It is easy for me to recognise individuals in situations that require people to wear similar clothes (e.g. suits; uniforms; swimwear) (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
18. At family gatherings I sometimes confuse individual family members (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
19. I find it easy to recognise celebrities in 'before-they-were-famous' photos; even if they have changed considerably (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
20. It is hard to recognise familiar people when I meet them out of context (e.g. meeting a work colleague unexpectedly while shopping) (1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree)
